author: Signal
content: In SCORE you had to refuel ships and log on all the time, but at least your APY was like 30%+.  If your APY for long term DAO staking turns out to be like 10%, making someone deal with that every day of their lives (or even every few days) to invest long term in your project is really just… punishment

author: Signal
content: You incentivize people to not want to do it, from a variety of angles.  Including the fact that if the website or UI itself is compromised even briefly the results could be unfortunate for a lot of people (more times having to visit the watering hole for a gazelle increases the odds of being eaten by a crocodile).

author: Signal
content: But maybe that’s the point.  I don’t know.

author: Signal
content: If you own 30% of POLIS and can easily stay on top of it and create a DAO that nobody likes the value proposition of being locked into long term, maybe 50% of circulating POLIS never stakes…

author: Signal
content: Now your 30% vote, is 60% and growing

author: Signal
content: Not at all an accusation, but an interesting thought experiment.  ATMTA is really the only giant group absolutely guaranteed to be in the DAO long term.

author: Signal
content: Depending on staked percentage of circulating supply, adding in their yield and stake maturity for picking the longest available window, they could theoretically maintain a single party majority for quite a long time.

author: Signal
content: Lots of very interesting questions about power, fairness, return on investment, and the mechanics for deciding all of this.

author: Signal
content: Either way that’s my full train of thought / rant

author: Hades
content:
We are mixing two things, one thing is not to bother the new gamer and the other is to please the old investors. The first thing may bother or not, you think that nobody is going to be bothered because there are people with an advantage in the game. Honestly, ok, I buy it, but... that advantage is going to be shit, precisely not to bother the new gamer (if the difference was substantial ATMTA would have a problem), then we enter the next point, if the advantage does not bother anyone and is minimal, what real value of compensation and support is ATMTA doing on the old ones? Then you will have the old ones pissed off because they are mostly investors and want to get their money back and make more money, since they bet on it. 
To do this exercise it is good not to think about yourself, because if I put myself in my shoes and that's it, I also prefer experience points and many things in the game, rather than a Skin (well I want both), but if I think of all investors who in the end are the bulk (80% surely) of the current community, you want to keep them happy and continue to invest and push the project.

author: [AEP] Sensei_Yamura
content: totally agree, what we are talking about is the type of reward, not that they receive it. If the reward is a cosmetic improvement that brings capital it is more feasible for ATMTA to implement it and has much less friction than an in-game mechanic that gives an advantage when the game starts at the same time for everyone.  The reward to an early investor if it is inexpensive is more understandable because of what they endured until the game starts, but when the game starts, it is harder to justify certain improvements in the mechanics.  That said, it sounds like I don't want that, but I threw this into the F.R. because I'm worried that in the end it will remain such a minimal improvement that no one will be satisfied. It's an opportunity to also claim something that will revalue our fleet no?

author: [AEP] WindSplitter1 [AEP]
content:
@[AEP] Moustacheride, @[AEP] Sensei_Yamura & @Hades raise interesting points.

Just my thoughts from a pre-SCORE ship owner: Do exactly like the ReBirth campaign. Provide an airdrop of in-game resources with utility, such as crew gear, structures, and even fungible assets.

From a game balance perspective, it might discourage prospects from joining further down the road if they hear a sizeable amount of vessels possess an inherent advantage, but if a crew must possess them, as they are doing with charms, I think it's a solid limiting factor (assuming crews cannot be specialized in multiple ships, as I believe it was discussed already.

As current and new players are able to acquire new fully tuned vessels well below the MSRP, thus are capable of aggressively building their fleets for the same price that it cost early adopters for just one ship, sometimes with a premium, larger investors might feel somewhat let down with this, which is understandable. But if we are to provide some sort of compensation (which ATMTA should, IMHO), the game balance must also be a concern.

For the sake of ensuring everyone has a comparable starting point, providing advantages such as more speed or HP, might not be the best approach.
It will be difficult to measure who should receive what and how many, but in-game resources have tangible and concrete use (as opposed to skins which, despite being exclusive, are of little practical worth), might be more appropriate.

Note that such resources will have to be managed efficiently by the players to be of any use so, only the best ones will be able to translate that into an advantage, while making them available elsewhere, but paid in ATLAS. My thesis is... resources for resources, to compensate the early player.

author: [AEP] Moustacheride
content:
The ratio applies to your voting weight. @BCM [wade.digital] can likely elaborate on the underlying mechanics, derived from the Tribeca framework. However, I'll comment that Tribeca itself is a derivative of the Curve model - so these are fairly standardized multipliers to the time lock.

Additionally, it's worth pointing out that we derive proportional share of reward emissions from voting weight/vePOLIS, and not from staked balance. So as I have noted in the past, your share of rewards will be comprised of both your staked balance and your lock duration. The multipliers don't directly determine rewards, per se, but they do influence your share, and transitively your reward calculation.

author: Xap✨ [STAR]
content: Quick comment about SCREAM expectations. We should be clear that V0 of scream will be missing a lot of features. We will see a number of iterations before a more enjoyable and competent version of SCREAM will emerge. Till then the members of the community should not pass judgement on success of SA based on what is delivered in V0 of SCREAM. This is a long journey and there will be a number of new players/users that need to educated about how games are developed using an iterative model. The education and support starts with people in this room. I foresee a flood of negative comments and FUD from existing and new people when SCREAM v0 comes out.
